task.name: Web Frontend Feature Parity
task.description: Fix critical WebSocket/state bugs and bring React/Vite frontend to feature parity with archived Python/DearPyGui implementation

vision_extraction.primary_goal: Achieve feature parity between web frontend and archived Python visualization
success_criteria: No WebSocket race conditions with multiple clients
UI state properly resets on reset/config change
All Python visualization features available in web UI
Comparison mode supports parallel protocol runs

context.gap_analysis_summary: Core grid/charts complete; overlays partial; belief viz, perspective mode, replay, comparison mode all missing; WebSocket has concurrency bugs

phases.name: Critical Fixes
phases.description: Fix bugs identified in code review that cause incorrect behavior
phases.exit_criteria: Multiple browser tabs work without race conditions; reset clears UI; config broadcasts

phases.name: Core Polish
phases.description: Fix remaining medium-priority issues from code review
phases.exit_criteria: PNG exports correct canvas; overlays match spec defaults; trade network stable

phases.name: Belief System
phases.description: Add belief visualization features from Python implementation
phases.exit_criteria: Belief panel shows selected agent beliefs; belief connections overlay works; use_beliefs toggleable

phases.name: Perspective Mode
phases.exit_criteria: Can view simulation from any agent's perspective with noisy observations

phases.name: Comparison Mode
phases.exit_criteria: Can run same seed with different protocols side-by-side

phases.name: Replay Mode
phases.description: Support loading and seeking through saved runs
phases.exit_criteria: Can load saved run, seek via timeline, step backward

phases.name: Finishing
phases.exit_criteria: Trade animations work; scenario browser functional; keyboard shortcuts active

features.name: WebSocket concurrency fix
features.description: Prevent overlapping simulation loops when multiple clients connect. Single simulation instance broadcasts to all observers.

functional: Opening 2 browser tabs does not cause double-stepping
All connected clients see same simulation state
Disconnecting one client does not affect others

features.name: Reset clears UI state
features.description: Reset message clears history, trade connections, trails in UI - not just server state

features.name: Config broadcast
features.description: Config changes broadcast to all connected clients, not just the one that made the change

functional: Config change in one tab reflects in other tabs

features.name: Dev script fix
features.description: Fix scripts/dev.sh to use correct server entry point

functional: scripts/dev.sh starts both server and frontend successfully

features.name: PNG export targets grid canvas
features.description: PNG export explicitly targets the grid canvas, not arbitrary first canvas

functional: PNG export captures grid canvas even with modals open

features.name: Overlay defaults alignment
features.description: Overlay toggles default to off per VISUALIZATION.md; remove non-functional heatmap or implement it

features.name: Trade network stability
features.description: Trade network doesn't fully rebuild D3 state every tick; reduce jitter

functional: Trade network updates smoothly without jitter on each tick

features.name: Trade connections pruning
features.description: Trade connections don't accumulate forever; prune old connections

functional: Trade connections limited to reasonable count (e.g., last 100)

features.name: Belief panel
features.description: Panel showing selected agent's beliefs: price belief (mean, variance, n), type beliefs list

functional: Shows type beliefs about other agents

features.name: Belief connections overlay
features.description: Overlay drawing lines between agents that hold beliefs about each other

functional: Toggle shows/hides belief connection lines on grid

features.name: use_beliefs UI toggle
features.description: Config modal includes toggle for use_beliefs parameter

functional: Can toggle use_beliefs on/off in config modal

features.name: Perspective mode toggle
features.description: Toggle to view simulation from a specific agent's perspective (what they can see)

features.name: Agent selector for perspective
features.description: Dropdown to select which agent's perspective to view

functional: Can select any agent from dropdown
Grid updates to show selected agent's view

features.name: Ground truth comparison
features.description: Toggle to show ground truth alongside agent's noisy observations

functional: Can compare what agent sees vs true state

features.name: Multi-simulation server support
features.description: Server supports creating/managing multiple simulation instances per session

functional: Client can create multiple simulations with different configs

features.name: Dual grid layout
features.description: UI layout showing two simulation grids side-by-side

features.name: Synchronized comparison controls
features.description: Play/pause/step controls affect both simulations simultaneously

functional: Single play button steps both sims

features.name: Comparison chart overlay
features.description: Welfare/trade charts overlay both simulations for comparison

features.name: Load saved run
features.description: Load RunData from JSON log file for replay

functional: Can select and load a saved run file

features.name: Timeline seeking
features.description: Slider to seek to any tick in loaded replay

functional: Dragging updates view to that tick

features.name: Step backward
features.description: Step-back button in replay mode

functional: Step-back button visible in replay mode

features.name: Trade animation
features.description: Animate trades with ~2 second visual effect showing exchange

features.name: Scenario browser
features.description: Browser for selecting pre-defined scenarios by complexity level

functional: Shows available scenarios grouped by level

features.name: Keyboard shortcuts
features.description: Space for play/pause, arrow keys for stepping

resolved.choice: Session-based simulation ownership - each WebSocket session can own 0-N simulations
resolved.rationale: Most flexible for comparison mode where one client needs multiple sims with different protocols. Client sends {action: 'create', config: {...}, label: 'nash'} to create sims.

resolved.choice: Client-side preload - client loads full RunData, seeks locally
resolved.rationale: Runs are typically <1000 ticks, data is small. Instant seeking, simpler server. Can optimize to server-side for very large runs later.

resolved.choice: Reset clears all UI state (history, trails, connections)
resolved.rationale: Matches user expectation of 'reset' meaning clean slate

escalation_triggers: Changes to core simulation engine (microecon/) without discussion
Test failures in existing test suite
